item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan issues tsunami warnings after 7.7 magnitude earthquake strikes northern coast
item.description: A powerful 7.7 earthquake hit off northern Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and evacuation orders. Meteorological agency warns of possible stronger aftershocks in the coming week.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canadian PM Carney says economic ties with US are weakness that must be corrected
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Canada must diversify trade relationships and reduce dependence on the US economy, signaling a major shift in Canadian economic policy amid tensions.

item.title: 🇳🇿 New Zealand declares state of emergency as severe floods hit Wellington region
item.description: Widespread flooding in Wellington has submerged vehicles, uprooted trees and triggered landslides. State of emergency declared as cleanup begins.

item.title: 🚨 Around 250 Rohingya refugees missing after boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
item.description: A vessel carrying Rohingya refugees capsized in the Andaman Sea, with approximately 250 people reported missing in the latest tragedy involving the persecuted minority.

item.title: 🇮🇳 At least 21 killed as bus crashes into Kashmir gorge
item.description: An overcrowded bus skidded off a mountain road and fell into a gorge in Indian-administered Kashmir, killing at least 21 passengers and injuring dozens more.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Surge 6% as US-Iran Ceasefire Falters, Stocks Retreat from Records
item.description: Global oil prices jumped 6% on fears the US-Iran ceasefire may collapse after the US Navy seized an Iranian cargo ship. Wall Street stocks pulled back from record highs as investors assessed the geopolitical risk.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Says Not Under Pressure on Iran Deal as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: President Trump stated he faces no pressure to rush an Iran deal despite the ceasefire expiring Wednesday. Iran says it has no plans for the next round of negotiations, casting doubt on Islamabad peace talks.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran Vows Retaliation After US Seizes Cargo Ship, Peace Talks in Doubt
item.description: Iran condemned the US Navy seizure of an Iranian-flagged tanker as armed piracy and vowed retaliation. The incident has cast doubt on whether the second round of peace talks in Islamabad will proceed.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Officer Deploys 343 Days on Front Line, Highlighting Manpower Crisis
item.description: A Ukrainian infantry officer spent 343 consecutive days on the front line, believed to be one of the longest continuous combat deployments. The case underscores Kyivs growing manpower challenges after years of war.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇫🇷 Poland and France Discuss Joint Nuclear Exercises as US Commitment Wavers
item.description: Polish PM Donald Tusk and French President Macron discussed joint nuclear deterrence exercises. Poland is exploring deeper defense ties with France amid concerns about US commitment to European security.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iranian president voices deep mistrust of US ahead of Islamabad peace talks
item.description: President Pezeshkian says historical mistrust of the US government remains as second round of Iran-US talks prepare to kick off in Pakistan. The comments come as the ceasefire deadline approaches.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇸🇩 Saudi crown prince hosts Sudan army chief as .5B arms deal stalls
item.description: MBS met with General Burhan in Jeddah amid reports a major Saudi-backed weapons deal faces delays. The visit highlights Saudi efforts to maintain influence in Sudans ongoing conflict.

item.title: ⚓🛡️ US Navy warns sailors to secure social media from Iranian online threats
item.description: The Navy issued security guidance to service members to protect electronic devices and accounts amid increased online threats linked to the Iran-Israel war.

item.title: 🇨🇳🤖 Pentagon deploys AI to vet China ties of university professors
item.description: After watchdog criticism of understaffing, the Defense Department is turning to artificial intelligence to screen Pentagon-funded researchers for potential espionage ties to Beijing.

item.title: 🇦🇫🛢️ African economies strain as Iran shuts Hormuz route again
item.description: African nations face renewed cost pressures after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again, disrupting vital shipping routes and threatening fuel imports across the continent.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel Entrenches Control Over South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israel warned residents to stay out of a border territory belt and not approach the Litani River area, deepening its grip on southern Lebanon despite the Hezbollah ceasefire. The move signals Israel's intent to maintain a security buffer zone indefinitely.

item.title: 🇮🇷🛢️ Gulf States Fear US-Iran Talks May Cement Tehran's Grip on Hormuz
item.description: Gulf Arab states worry that US-Iran negotiations may only achieve reopening the Strait of Hormuz while leaving Iran with enhanced leverage over the critical waterway. Former Russian President Medvedev's warning crystallized regional fears about Tehran's "golden" control.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇺🇸🇨🇳 Philippines and US Launch Military Exercises Near Taiwan
item.description: Philippine and US forces began annual military exercises featuring maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan. Manila's military chief said the drills would test readiness under "real-world conditions" amid regional tensions with China.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇷🇺 Germany Summons Russian Ambassador Over Direct Threats
item.description: Berlin summoned the Russian ambassador to condemn what it called "direct threats" against targets in Germany, escalating diplomatic tensions between the two countries. The move reflects growing friction between Russia and European nations.

item.title: 🇵🇸🕊️ Board of Peace Envoy Sees Progress on Gaza Disarmament Plan
item.description: The Board of Peace's lead envoy for Gaza said he was "fairly optimistic" that a plan for disarmament of Hamas and other militant groups in Gaza can be agreed, but cautioned it will take time. The comments come as over 60 nations met in Brussels to discuss Gaza stability.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇸🇦 China's Xi Jinping Calls for Hormuz to Remain Open in Phone Call With Saudi Crown Prince
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping called for normal passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz to be maintained in a phone call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman. Xi urged an immediate ceasefire between the US and Iran and stressed that the waterway's reopening was in the 'common interests of all countries.'

item.title: 🇮🇷🇵🇰 Iran May Join Peace Talks After Pakistan Works to End US Blockade, Official Says
item.description: Iran is considering attending peace talks with the United States in Pakistan after Islamabad moved to end the US naval blockade of Iran's ports, a senior Iranian official told Reuters. The development could revive negotiations ahead of the ceasefire deadline on Wednesday.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇱 Trump Says Israel Never Talked Him Into War With Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said Israel did not persuade him to attack Iran, rejecting reports that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu influenced his decision. The statement comes amid criticism from right-wing commentators questioning the war's origins.

item.title: ⛪ Israel Condemns Soldier's Desecration of Crucifix in Southern Lebanon
item.description: A photo showing the desecration of a crucifix smashed by an Israeli soldier in a southern Lebanese Christian village drew widespread condemnation from Israeli leaders, the United States and church leaders. The incident has damaged Israel's support among evangelical Christians.

item.title: 🌍 Battle-Scarred Developing Nations Seek Path Out of 'Permacrisis' at IMF Meetings
item.description: Developing country policymakers left the IMF-World Bank meetings frustrated that successive external shocks are derailing efforts to tackle high debt, reform economies and deliver better lives. The Iran war's energy disruptions have hit the world's poorest nations hardest.
